summary: PR 1366 finished matching sysdolphin/baselib/displayfunc.c by replacing the remaining assembly object with C, primarily through implementing the billboard matrix helpers that had been NOT_IMPLEMENTED. The build metadata was updated to classify displayfunc.c as Matching and to compile src/sysdolphin/baselib/displayfunc.c.o instead of asm/sysdolphin/baselib/displayfunc.s.o, deleting the 1718-line assembly fallback.
what_changed: Deleted asm/sysdolphin/baselib/displayfunc.s, removing the assembly fallback for displayfunc.
Changed configure.py from Object(NonMatching, "sysdolphin/baselib/displayfunc.c") to Object(Matching, "sysdolphin/baselib/displayfunc.c").
Changed obj_files.mk to build $(BUILD_DIR)/src/sysdolphin/baselib/displayfunc.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/sysdolphin/baselib/displayfunc.s.o.
Implemented mkVBillBoardMtx, mkHBillBoardMtx, and mkBillBoardMtx in src/sysdolphin/baselib/displayfunc.c, replacing NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs.
Added global Vec3 constants zOne = { 0, 0, 1 }, yOne = { 0, 1, 0 }, and zOne2 = { 0, 0, 1 } used by the billboard matrix routines.
Added displayfunc.h declarations for HSD_JObjMakePositionMtx, HSD_JObjDispSub, HSD_JObjDispDObj, and HSD_JObjFindSkeleton.
Added HSD_MtxColMagFloat inline helper to mtx.h, computing sqrtf of a matrix column length.
smart_moves: Used small matrix/vector helper abstractions such as HSD_MtxColVec, HSD_MtxSetColVec, HSD_MtxColMagFloat, VECCrossProduct, and VECMag instead of manually spelling every matrix load/store from the assembly.
Separated the three billboard cases into mkVBillBoardMtx, mkHBillBoardMtx, and mkBillBoardMtx, preserving the original displayfunc structure while making the math readable.

Preserved column-wise matrix construction explicitly: scale factors are derived from source matrix column magnitudes, axes are produced by cross products, and destination matrix columns are filled in the same column layout.

Kept duplicate-looking unit vector globals zOne and zOne2 rather than collapsing them, which is likely important for matching data references/order given the deleted assembly had separate data symbols for unit vector constants.
Introduced HSD_MtxColMagFloat using sqrtf, matching the float-oriented codegen expected by the billboard helpers instead of relying only on the preexisting HSD_MtxColMag helper.
decomp_lessons: When matching matrix-heavy code, column helper functions can express the original loads and stores cleanly while still preserving codegen-sensitive ordering.
Do not aggressively deduplicate identical constants during matching; separate globals such as zOne and zOne2 may be required to preserve symbol/data layout or distinct references.

For billboard routines, the order of cross products and normalization divisions is matching-sensitive. This PR keeps the sequence visible: choose axes based on JOBJ_PBILLBOARD, cross to derive the remaining axis, divide source scale by vector magnitude, then write destination columns.
A fully matched source file must be reflected in both build classification and object selection: configure.py should mark the source Matching and obj_files.mk should switch from asm .s.o to src .c.o.
Adding prototypes to the relevant header can accompany source matching when previously-local or undeclared display functions are now needed by other decompiled code.
naming_conventions: Billboard helper names use lower camel/prefix style already present in the file: mkVBillBoardMtx, mkHBillBoardMtx, mkBillBoardMtx, mkRBillBoardMtx.
Matrix helper naming follows HSD_Mtx* convention, with the new HSD_MtxColMagFloat mirroring HSD_MtxColMag while making float behavior explicit.
JObj display API declarations use HSD_JObj* names, including HSD_JObjDispSub, HSD_JObjDispDObj, HSD_JObjMakePositionMtx, and HSD_JObjFindSkeleton.
Temporary vector variables in the matched C use compact axis names ax, ay, az and pos, matching the mathematical role of matrix columns.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced NOT_IMPLEMENTED stubs with C that matched enough to remove the entire displayfunc.s assembly file.
Used sqrtf through HSD_MtxColMagFloat and direct sqrtf in mkHBillBoardMtx for float math consistent with the assembly's single-precision matrix calculations.
Maintained explicit destination stores such as dst[0][0], dst[1][0], dst[2][0] for generated matrix columns, which helps control store order and codegen.
Used pointer selection in mkBillBoardMtx, assigning az either to &pos or &zOne2, matching the branch-dependent source vector behavior without duplicating later stores.
Handled JOBJ_PBILLBOARD branches separately, including the perspective billboard paths using position-derived cross products and the non-perspective paths using fixed unit vectors.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review bodies were present in the provided PR slice, so there is no explicit reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Search for later changes touching zOne, yOne, or zOne2 to confirm whether duplicate unit-vector globals were retained for data matching.
Search for HSD_MtxColMagFloat users to see whether this float-specific helper became a reusable matching tactic outside displayfunc.
Search later displayfunc.c PRs for mkRBillBoardMtx or HSD_JObjDispSub changes to understand whether this PR fully stabilized the file.
Check whether displayfunc.h prototype additions were later moved or refined as more sysdolphin/baselib files matched.
